name: deploy
context: Deploy projects to Vercel — git push, CLI deploy, preview deployments, and production promotions
info: This skill covers the full deployment lifecycle on Vercel. Deployments can be triggered by pushing to a connected Git repository (automatic), running `vercel` or `vercel --prod` from the CLI, or calling the Vercel REST API. Every deployment is immutable and gets a unique URL. Preview deployments are created for non-production branches and pull requests; production deployments serve traffic on the project's production domain. Use `vercel promote` to promote a preview deployment to production without rebuilding. Force deploys bypass the build cache. Configure build settings, output directory, and framework detection in `vercel.json` or project settings.
body:
# Project Deployment

Deploying to Vercel creates an immutable build artifact served from a global CDN. Each deployment receives a unique URL (`https://<project>-<hash>-<org>.vercel.app`). Preview deployments are created for branches; production deployments serve the primary domain.

## Deployment Methods

### Git Push

The simplest method. Push to a connected repository and Vercel builds automatically:

```bash
git push origin feature-branch   # creates preview deployment
git push origin main             # creates production deployment
```

### CLI Deploy

```bash
# Preview deployment (default)
vercel

# Production deployment
vercel --prod

# Force deploy (skip cache)
vercel --force

# Deploy specific directory
vercel ./dist --prod
```

### API Deploy

```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.vercel.com/v13/deployments" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $VERCEL_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "my-project", "gitSource": {"type": "github", "ref": "main", "repoId": "12345"}}'
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | Authentication token for CLI and API |
| `VERCEL_ORG_ID` | Organization/team scope |
| `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` | Target project identifier |

## Build Settings

Configure in `vercel.json` or project dashboard:

```json
{
  "buildCommand": "npm run build",
  "outputDirectory": "dist",
  "installCommand": "npm install",
  "framework": "nextjs"
}
```

## Production Promote

Promote any deployment to production without rebuilding:

```bash
vercel promote <deployment-url>
```

This instantly routes production traffic to the specified deployment. Useful for rollbacks — promote a previous known-good deployment.

## Deployment States

| State | Meaning |
|-------|----------|
| `QUEUED` | Waiting for build capacity |
| `BUILDING` | Build in progress |
| `READY` | Successfully deployed, serving traffic |
| `ERROR` | Build or deployment failed |
| `CANCELED` | Deployment was canceled |

sections.title: Git-based deployment
sections.body: Connect a GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository to enable automatic deployments on push. Pushing to the production branch (default: `main`) triggers a production deployment. All other branches create preview deployments. Pull requests get deploy previews with status checks and bot comments containing the preview URL. Configure ignored build steps to skip unnecessary builds.

sections.title: CLI deployment
sections.body: Run `vercel` for preview or `vercel --prod` for production. Use `--force` to bypass build cache. Use `--env KEY=VALUE` to override environment variables for a single deployment. The CLI reads `VERCEL_TOKEN`, `VERCEL_ORG_ID`, and `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` from the environment for non-interactive (CI) usage. See [[vercel/env-vars]] for variable management.

sections.title: Preview deployments
sections.body: Every non-production deployment is a preview. Preview deployments use environment variables scoped to `preview` (see [[vercel/env-vars#scopes]]). They get unique URLs and are accessible to team members. Preview URLs follow the pattern `https://<project>-<hash>-<org>.vercel.app`. Use preview deployments for staging, QA, and pull request reviews.

sections.title: Production promotion
sections.body: Use `vercel promote <url>` to route production traffic to any existing deployment without rebuilding. This is instant and enables zero-downtime rollbacks. The promoted deployment inherits production [[vercel/env-vars]]. View promotion history in the project dashboard. Use `vercel rollback` as a shortcut to promote the previous production deployment.

sections.title: Build settings
sections.body: Configure `buildCommand`, `outputDirectory`, `installCommand`, and `framework` in `vercel.json` or the project dashboard. Vercel auto-detects frameworks (Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, etc.) and applies optimal defaults. Override Node.js version with the `engines.node` field in `package.json`. Use `vercel.json` rewrites and redirects for routing configuration.
